speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: The noise is round about us.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Let us from it.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: What pleasure, sir, find we in life, to lock it

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: From action and adventure?

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Nay, what hope

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Have we in hiding us? This way the Romans

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Must or for Britons slay us or receive us

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: For barbarous and unnatural revolts

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: During their use, and slay us after.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Sons,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: We’ll higher to the mountains, there secure us.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: To the King’s party there’s no going. Newness

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Of Cloten’s death—we being not known, not mustered

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Among the bands—may drive us to a render

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Where we have lived, and so extort from ’s that

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Which we have done, whose answer would be death

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Drawn on with torture.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: This is, sir, a doubt

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: In such a time nothing becoming you

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Nor satisfying us.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: It is not likely

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: That when they hear the Roman horses neigh,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Behold their quartered fires, have both their eyes

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: And ears so cloyed importantly as now,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: That they will waste their time upon our note,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: To know from whence we are.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: O, I am known

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Of many in the army. Many years,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Though Cloten then but young, you see not wore him

speaker: BELARIUS
text: From my remembrance. And besides, the King

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Hath not deserved my service nor your loves,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Who find in my exile the want of breeding,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The certainty of this hard life, aye hopeless

speaker: BELARIUS
text: To have the courtesy your cradle promised,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: But to be still hot summer’s tanlings and

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The shrinking slaves of winter.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Than be so

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Better to cease to be. Pray, sir, to th’ army.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: I and my brother are not known; yourself

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: So out of thought, and thereto so o’ergrown,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Cannot be questioned.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: By this sun that shines,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: I’ll thither. What thing is ’t that I never

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Did see man die, scarce ever looked on blood

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: But that of coward hares, hot goats, and venison!

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Never bestrid a horse save one that had

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: A rider like myself, who ne’er wore rowel

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Nor iron on his heel! I am ashamed

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: To look upon the holy sun, to have

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: The benefit of his blest beams, remaining

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: So long a poor unknown.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: By heavens, I’ll go!

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: If you will bless me, sir, and give me leave,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: I’ll take the better care, but if you will not,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: The hazard therefore due fall on me by

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: The hands of Romans.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: So say I. Amen.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: No reason I—since of your lives you set

speaker: BELARIUS
text: So slight a valuation—should reserve

speaker: BELARIUS
text: My cracked one to more care. Have with you, boys!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: If in your country wars you chance to die,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: That is my bed, too, lads, and there I’ll lie.

speaker: BELARIUS
text:
Lead, lead.
                 
                Aside
                .
               The time seems long; their blood thinks scorn

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Till it fly out and show them princes born.
